name: Shakespearean English

formality.description: Constructed language or stylistic variant — no formality system. Tone is defined entirely by the register preset.

standard.label: Standard Shakespearean
standard.description: Early Modern English with thee/thou forms and period-appropriate vocabulary.
standard.prompt: Shakespearean English. Use Early Modern English conventions: thee/thou for second person, -eth/-est verb endings (e.g., 'thou hast', 'it doth'), period-appropriate vocabulary. Maintain comprehensibility — the user should understand the meaning even if the style is archaic. UI labels can be shorter; descriptions get the full treatment.

notes: Register-only conlang — English with Early Modern English style transformation.
